source_id: token-overflow-test
title: Comprehensive Cloud Computing Glossary
category: infrastructure
---

# Comprehensive Cloud Computing Glossary

## Overview

This glossary provides definitions for cloud computing terminology. It is intentionally large to test the pipeline's token budget enforcement (EXIT_TOKEN_OVERFLOW, code 5).

### Availability Zone (Variant 1)

A physically isolated data center within a cloud region, connected to other zones via low-latency links. This is entry number 1 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

### Auto Scaling (Variant 2)

Automatic adjustment of compute resources based on demand metrics such as CPU utilization or request count. This is entry number 2 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

### Blob Storage (Variant 3)

Object storage service for unstructured data such as images, videos, documents, and backups. This is entry number 3 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

### CIDR Block (Variant 4)

Classless Inter-Domain Routing notation for specifying IP address ranges used in VPC and subnet configuration. This is entry number 4 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

### Container Orchestration (Variant 5)

Automated management of containerized application lifecycle including deployment, scaling, and networking. This is entry number 5 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

### Data Lake (Variant 6)

Centralized repository that stores structured and unstructured data at any scale for analytics and machine learning. This is entry number 6 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

### Edge Computing (Variant 7)

Processing data near the source of data generation rather than in a centralized data center. This is entry number 7 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

### Fault Tolerance (Variant 8)

The ability of a system to continue operating correctly when one or more of its components fail. This is entry number 8 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

### Global Load Balancer (Variant 9)

Traffic distribution service that routes requests across multiple regions based on latency, health, and capacity. This is entry number 9 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

### Horizontal Scaling (Variant 10)

Adding more instances of a service to handle increased load, as opposed to vertical scaling which adds resources to existing instances. This is entry number 10 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

### Identity Federation (Variant 11)

Authentication mechanism that allows users to access cloud resources using credentials from an external identity provider. This is entry number 11 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

### Just-in-Time Access (Variant 12)

Security model where elevated permissions are granted temporarily and automatically revoked after a defined period. This is entry number 12 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

### Key Management Service (Variant 13)

Managed service for creating, storing, and controlling access to encryption keys used to protect data. This is entry number 13 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

### Lambda Architecture (Variant 14)

Data processing architecture that combines batch processing for completeness with stream processing for low latency. This is entry number 14 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

### Managed Database (Variant 15)

Database service where the cloud provider handles provisioning, patching, backup, and high availability. This is entry number 15 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

### Network ACL (Variant 16)

Stateless firewall rules applied at the subnet level to control inbound and outbound traffic by IP address and port. This is entry number 16 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

### Object Versioning (Variant 17)

Storage feature that maintains multiple versions of an object, enabling recovery from accidental overwrites or deletions. This is entry number 17 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

### Private Endpoint (Variant 18)

Network interface that connects a VPC to a cloud service without routing traffic through the public internet. This is entry number 18 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

### Queue Service (Variant 19)

Managed message queuing service that decouples producers from consumers and provides at-least-once delivery guarantees. This is entry number 19 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

### Resource Tagging (Variant 20)

Metadata labels applied to cloud resources for cost allocation, access control, automation, and organizational tracking. This is entry number 20 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

### Availability Zone (Variant 21)

A physically isolated data center within a cloud region, connected to other zones via low-latency links. This is entry number 21 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

### Global Load Balancer (Variant 2049)

Traffic distribution service that routes requests across multiple regions based on latency, health, and capacity. This is entry number 2049 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

### Horizontal Scaling (Variant 2050)

Adding more instances of a service to handle increased load, as opposed to vertical scaling which adds resources to existing instances. This is entry number 2050 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

Authentication mechanism that allows users to access cloud resources using credentials from an external identity provider. This is entry number 2051 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

Security model where elevated permissions are granted temporarily and automatically revoked after a defined period. This is entry number 2052 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

### Global Load Balancer (Variant 2589)

Traffic distribution service that routes requests across multiple regions based on latency, health, and capacity. This is entry number 2589 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

Adding more instances of a service to handle increased load, as opposed to vertical scaling which adds resources to existing instances. This is entry number 2590 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

Authentication mechanism that allows users to access cloud resources using credentials from an external identity provider. This is entry number 2591 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

Security model where elevated permissions are granted temporarily and automatically revoked after a defined period. This is entry number 2592 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

### Global Load Balancer (Variant 3129)

Traffic distribution service that routes requests across multiple regions based on latency, health, and capacity. This is entry number 3129 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

Adding more instances of a service to handle increased load, as opposed to vertical scaling which adds resources to existing instances. This is entry number 3130 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

Authentication mechanism that allows users to access cloud resources using credentials from an external identity provider. This is entry number 3131 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

Security model where elevated permissions are granted temporarily and automatically revoked after a defined period. This is entry number 3132 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

Traffic distribution service that routes requests across multiple regions based on latency, health, and capacity. This is entry number 3669 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

### Queue Service (Variant 3939)

Managed message queuing service that decouples producers from consumers and provides at-least-once delivery guarantees. This is entry number 3939 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

### Resource Tagging (Variant 3940)

Metadata labels applied to cloud resources for cost allocation, access control, automation, and organizational tracking. This is entry number 3940 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

### Availability Zone (Variant 3941)

A physically isolated data center within a cloud region, connected to other zones via low-latency links. This is entry number 3941 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

### Auto Scaling (Variant 3942)

Automatic adjustment of compute resources based on demand metrics such as CPU utilization or request count. This is entry number 3942 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

Object storage service for unstructured data such as images, videos, documents, and backups. This is entry number 3943 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

Classless Inter-Domain Routing notation for specifying IP address ranges used in VPC and subnet configuration. This is entry number 3944 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

Automated management of containerized application lifecycle including deployment, scaling, and networking. This is entry number 3945 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

Centralized repository that stores structured and unstructured data at any scale for analytics and machine learning. This is entry number 3946 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

### Edge Computing (Variant 3947)

Processing data near the source of data generation rather than in a centralized data center. This is entry number 3947 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

### Fault Tolerance (Variant 3948)

The ability of a system to continue operating correctly when one or more of its components fail. This is entry number 3948 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

Traffic distribution service that routes requests across multiple regions based on latency, health, and capacity. This is entry number 3949 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

### Horizontal Scaling (Variant 3950)

Adding more instances of a service to handle increased load, as opposed to vertical scaling which adds resources to existing instances. This is entry number 3950 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

Authentication mechanism that allows users to access cloud resources using credentials from an external identity provider. This is entry number 3951 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.

Security model where elevated permissions are granted temporarily and automatically revoked after a defined period. This is entry number 3952 in the glossary, included to ensure the document exceeds the model's context window for token budget enforcement testing.
